# Machine Learning for Medical Diagnosis with Explainable AI

## Overview

End-to-end **Machine Learning pipeline for medical diagnosis** using the Breast Cancer Wisconsin dataset.
The project focuses on accurate prediction, model interpretability, and reliable probability estimation for healthcare AI applications.

Key features:

* End-to-end ML workflow
* Model comparison (Logistic, Random Forest, XGBoost)
* Hyperparameter tuning (GridSearchCV)
* Class imbalance handling (SMOTE, class weights)
* Explainable AI (SHAP)
* Calibration analysis
* Confidence / uncertainty estimation
* Counterfactual explanations (DiCE)

---

## Dataset

Breast Cancer Wisconsin Dataset (sklearn)

* Samples: 569
* Features: 30
* Target:

  * 0 → malignant
  * 1 → benign

Common benchmark dataset for **medical ML classification**.

---

## Pipeline

* Data preprocessing & EDA
* Train / test split (stratified)
* Feature scaling
* Model training & comparison
* Hyperparameter tuning
* Imbalance handling
* SHAP explainability
* Calibration curve
* Confidence estimation
* Counterfactual explanations

---

## Models

* Logistic Regression
* Random Forest
* XGBoost

Libraries:

scikit-learn, xgboost, pandas, numpy, shap, imbalanced-learn, dice-ml, matplotlib, seaborn

---

## Evaluation

* Accuracy / Precision / Recall / F1
* ROC-AUC
* Cross-validation
* Confusion matrix
* Calibration curve

ROC-AUC used due to class imbalance in medical datasets.

---

## Highlights

* Explainable AI with SHAP
* Hyperparameter tuning with CV
* SMOTE for imbalance
* Calibration for probability reliability
* Counterfactual explanations
* Confidence estimation for safe predictions

---

## Run

pip install numpy pandas scikit-learn matplotlib seaborn shap xgboost imbalanced-learn dice-ml

jupyter notebook → notebook1.ipynb

---

## Skills

Machine Learning • Data Science • Python • scikit-learn • XGBoost • SHAP • Explainable AI • Calibration • Imbalanced data • Hyperparameter tuning • Healthcare AI
